Upon completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, proper disposal of all obsolete and unserviceable properties shall be undertaken pursuant to the instructions of Section 79 of PD 1445 and procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296.
Require the Accounting Unit to drop from the books all items in the IIRUP duly supported with Journal entry vouchers (JEVs). Reflect the accurate amount of PPE in the financial statement at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend Management to conduct inventory of insurable properties and submit listing to GSIS for evaluation/appraisal of the insurance coverage and comply to RA 656 and Memorandum Circular No. 634 dated May 10, 1973 of the Office of the President.

The municipality shall appropriate total amount for the payment of insurance coverage to all insurable assets of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Accounted PPEs already insured to GSIS while unvalidated PPE still to be processed
observations.reason: Majority of Motor vehicles are insured with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to keep and maintain a check register where all checks drawn for the day are immediately recorded in chronological sequence and where the rightful claimants are required to acknowledge receipt of the check pursuant to Section 42 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Petty Cash Fund in an Imprest Fund System pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.  The foregoing has to be recorded in the Petty Cash Fund account and shall be used solely for the intended purpose.

We recommend that management comply on the documentary requirement for the filing of the request for relief from accountability on the damaged/lost properties in compliance to Section 73 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 93-404. Otherwise, the accountable officer shall not be relieved from accountability nor be allowed credit for the loss during the settlement of his account.

We recommend that management require the Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to book/recognize payables in accordance with Section 9 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I. Likewise, see to it that it is supported with valid documentary requirements pursuant to Section 157 of the GAAM.  Also, record these payables in accordance with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards for an accurate presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the cause of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of the agency in order to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.

We recommend that management require the Local Finance Committee and the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare a budget based on a reasonable estimate of income so as not to incur overdraft or loss in its market operations. Likewise, minimize the expenses in relation to the said enterprise to at least break even by limiting total disbursements to fifty percent (50%) of the uncollected estimated revenue accruing to the fund in addition to the actual collections, provided that no cash overdraft shall be incurred, pursuant to Section 158 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume I, in order not to incur net loss of current income.

We recommend that Management properly monitor and control the grant and payment of monetization of leave credits to LGU officials and employees in accordance with the provisions of CSC MC Circular No. 41, series of 1998, to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.  Henceforth, limit the grant of monetization to employees who have substantial vacation and sick leave credits balances to qualify for availment of the monetary benefit.

We recommend to the Municipal General Services Officer to update the list of properties that needs to be insured and submit the same to GSIS and the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funding for the insurance coverage of all its insurable assets as required under Republic Act No.  656.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Engineer, signatories of the project plan, the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract (ABC), submit explanation on the overestimated quantity of carpentry items as detailed in the 3rd paragraph of this Audit Observation Memorandum. Henceforth, it is recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare accurate project cost estimate in the future projects.

In view thereof we recommend that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of the priority development projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund during the year the funds are actually appropriated in order to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and the desired outcomes from the said programs or projects

We recommend that management require the municipal officials involve in the disbursement process to evaluate its payment procedures and institute strategies and corrective measures on the lapses without deviating from existing laws, rules and regulations to minimize the delay in the settlement/payment of obligations. Also, management has to facilitate the preparation and evaluation of the supporting documents of the gasoline consumption for the period July to December 2021 and prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommend that Management create an Internal Audit Service (IAS) Unit that would handle the internal control functions of the agency pursuant to the above-mentioned rules and regulations. Refer to the Philippine Government Internal Audit Manual and the National Guidelines on Internal Control System as guide in strengthening the internal control system of the LGU.

In view of the forgoing, we recommend that management use the prescribed format and provide the complete data of the Annual Procurement Plan in compliance with GPPB Circular 08-2015 in order to properly guide the municipality on its procurement process.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to Submit a Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in the prescribed format to the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day of the month, through the LDRRMC and Local Development Councils (LCDs) as required under Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-02 and IPSAS 1 Section 127(c).

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the transfers of the agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund pursuant to Section 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management require the concerned municipal officials to comply the accounting and reporting guidelines prescribed by COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 14, 2014 to properly monitor the receipt and distribution of relief goods as well as accounting of all donated PPEs.

In view hereof, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund books and record it in Trust Liability-DRRM account as required under Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to revert to the General Fund the Accounts Payable that are outstanding for two years or more and without valid claims, in compliance with DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 99-6 and the provisions of PD No. 1445, and to submit explanation on the non-reversion of the account booked up for more than two years.

Accounts Payables recorded in the books have to be supported with complete documentations as basis in establishing the existence and accuracy of the reported amount balances.  The Accounting Unit have to see to it that all required documents will be kept intact prior to the recording of payables and avoid invalid obligations.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a letter addressed to CRU requiring submission of billing statements/collection letters with complete documentations based on the LGU’s records for possible payment only for the current year transactions/payables.

We recommend that the  Municipal Mayor designate personnel/committee  to undertake the actual inventory count of all LGU’s PPE and prepare the  inventory report for submission to  COA for validation.

1.a	Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) were not prepared/renewed contrary to Section 492& 42 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I, and II to pinpoint accountability and responsibility on the whereabouts of the properties.
Section 42 of  the NGAS Manual, Volume II, provides :
Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) – the acknowledgement receipt for equipment  shall be used to report on the receipt of property  and equipment for official use issued from the property officer.
“Section 492. Equipment issued by the property officer for official use of officials and employees shall be covered by Memorandum Receipt for Equipment (MR) which shall be renewed every January of the third year after issue. xxx.”
It was noted that issued Acknowledgement Receipt for  Equipment (ARE) to the end-users were not updated, while some issued properties  were not covered by  the ARE  thus, pinpointing of responsibility and accountability of lost items could not be established.

We recommend that  the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Office (GSO) to issue ARE to all end-users of any property issued and renew its ARE every three(3) years to update  recording and accountability. Transfer of property from one personnel to another has to be monitored , and issuance of the ARE will be immediately prepared after the cancellation of the previous receipt issued.

We recommend that:
The Local Chief Executive in the absence of General Services Office (GSO)  require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office/concerned personnel to conduct physical inventory taking of all LGU’s properties  and identify all  unserviceable units/items thru  the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable  Property(IIRUP)  and submit  to COA for review and validation.
Upon completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, proper disposal of all obsolete and unserviceable properties shall be undertaken pursuant to the instructions of Section 79 of PD 1445 and procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296.
Require the Accounting Unit to drop from the books all items reported in the IIRUP duly supported with Journal entry vouchers (JEVs). Reflect the accurate amount of PPE in the financial statement at year-end.

We recommend that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to locate necessary documents to substantiate the amount booked up as Due to Officers and Employees and submit explanation on the existence of the account Due to O&E for ten years’ time without complete documentation. Otherwise, make necessary adjustments if proven non-existing payables, by reverting obligated amounts to unappropriated surplus. Comply Section 111 of P.D. 1445.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accounting Unit to identify fully paid and completed projects funded from outside sources and return the unexpended balances to the source agencies. Cull out/housekeep all records which could no longer be substantiated with complete documentation and effect proper adjusting entries to arrive at the accurate and correct account balances as of year-end. Ascertain that liquidation reports were submitted to Source Agency in compliance with COA Circular 2004-008 and 94-013.

We recommend that the Management will communicate and require the depository banks to adjust particular transactions as identified by the Municipal Accountant to arrive at the reconciled balances per bank against per books.   Further, the Municipal Accountant make follow-up on the immediate response/action of the depository bank as well as take up the necessary adjustments on the reconciling items per books and reflect the reconciled balances in the books in a given period.

We recommend that stale checks still in the hands of the Municipal Treasurer should be cancelled and replaced as prescribed in the guidelines set forth in Section 59 of NGAS manual, Volume 1.  Stale checks that are already released to the payees shall only be cancelled and replaced upon presentation of the stale checks.  Meanwhile, the Treasurer should send a written notice to the payee to verify the existence of said checks.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant review the composition of the Accounts Payable to determine the valid claims, ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required documents to reflect only the true and valid payables of the Municipality. Submit the JEV for establishment/setting up of A/P with its related documents in accordance with Sec.6.05 of COA Circular No.95-006.

We recommend that the Management require the immediate liquidation of fund transfer through demand letters and monitor/follow-up compliance thereof. Otherwise, Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code states in unequivocal terms that the failure of a public officer to have duly forthcoming any public funds or property with which he is chargeable upon demand by any duly authorized officer shall be prima facie evidence that he has put such missing funds or property to personal use.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of MAO determine the existence and valuation of the Breeding Stocks and to present it based on either fair value less cost to sell or its cost less any accumulated depreciation and any impairment losses at reporting date to give an accurate valuation of the face of the Statement of Financial Position and to report any gain or loss in the Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommend Management to conduct inventory of insurable properties and submit listing to GSIS for evaluation/appraisal of  the  insurance coverage and comply  to RA 656 and Memorandum Circular No. 634 dated May 10, 1973 of the Office of the President.
The municipality shall appropriate total amount for the payment of insurance coverage to all insurable assets of the LGU.

We recommend that the management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRM focal person to adhere to the existing guidelines on the accounting and reporting of 5%Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.

Quantify/reconcile the difference between the reported amount and projects implemented as prepared by the two concerned offices and affect the reconciliation thereby.

We recommend that the concerned municipal officials closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% Development Fund and maximize utilization of the said fund to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.

We recommend that the Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to evaluate and re-assess the projects covered in the continuing appropriations if the same are still responsive to existing needs of its constituents, otherwise, reprogram the funds to more viable and significant projects in accordance with the socio-economic needs of the Municipality.
We also recommend that Management direct the MPDO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to fast track the implementation of the same so that any cause of suspension and/or problems in the course of implementation can be immediately addressed.

We recommend that the Budget Officer of the LGU during the deliberation of budget proposals considers the budget for all  mandatory programs, projects and activities aligned by the LGU and National Offices and ensure that corresponding appropriations be included in the annual budget in this case , the 1%  IRA of the municipality which is intended for LCPC .The concerned LGU personnel task to formulate and adopt  a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention as mandated has  to  oversee its proper preparation and implementation.

a. Scrutinize the importance of  hiring additional workforce who shall render administrative, janitorial and messenger’s works which would be a duplication of the regular employee handling  similar functions and responsibilities.

We recommend that:
The agency head should require the concerned personnel to conduct physical inventory taking and submit the report to COA for review and validation.
Property officer should prepare individual ARE for every property issued and renew the records  every 3 years thereafter pursuant to Section 42 of the NGAS  manual, to easily pinpoint responsibility and accountability.
Transfer of property from one personnel to another should be monitored, and issuance of the ARE will be immediately prepared after the cancellation of the previous receipt issued.
Inventory Report should be updated and cross-checked to the prior year’s report to ensure correctness, completeness and accuracy of data.
Maintain Property Ledger Cards of all PPE accounts to facilitate identification, monitoring and validation of items/units reflected in the inventory report.

We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to follow the steps prescribed in the COA Circular 97-001 in writing-off dormant/payable accounts if warranted and monitor prompt payments to creditors. Submit explanation/s on the non-payment to the concerned creditor for the amount aged beyond 360 days.

We recommend that the Budget Officer of the LGU during the deliberation of budget proposals considers the budget for all  mandatory programs, projects and activities aligned by the LGU and National Offices and ensure that corresponding appropriations be included in the annual budget in this case , the 1% IRA of the municipality.

We recommend that the LGU/Budget Officer appropriate/include in their budget the equivalent amount of 5% from their annual budget for the GAD related activities only and designate a Focal Person who will oversee in the implementation, reporting and compliance to all GAD related/existing   regulations.

We recommend that the management coordinate with GAD Focal Person to establish and maintain GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation, in compliance with PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-001

We recommend that management:
Stop hiring of additional workforce who shall render administrative, janitorial and messenger’s works which would be a duplication of the regular employee handling similar functions and responsibilities.
Require the concerned department head to submit activity designs, including project’s success indicators to substantiate and justify the necessity of hiring the JOs and payment of wages in their office.
Instruct the Human Resource Management Officer to review the existing plantilla position of the municipality  to equate the actual JO personnel against additional workload. Prepare activity design/work to be performed by JO’s and require submission of accomplishment report duly approved by the concerned head of office. Comply Item 1.2.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 24, 2012.

We recommend that the management require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to prepare the annual LDRRMO plan and budget as basis in the allocation of the LDRRM. Implement all proposed pre-disaster preparedness programs and projects for CY 2018 within the time frame so that the intended purpose will be attained/achieved as provided in RA 10121. Submit the 2018 LDRRMO Plans and Budget.

We recommend that the management require the concerned officials to adhere to the provisions of DILG Circular 2011-59 and COA Circular No.85-55A to prevent the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures.

We recommend Management to conduct inventory of insurable properties and submit listing to GSIS for evaluation/appraisal of the insurance coverage and comply to RA 656 and Memorandum Circular No. 634 dated May 10, 1973 of the Office of the President.

Instruct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer and all other concerned personnel to work for the immediate titling, recording or transfer of ownership of all land belonging to the LGU.
Record all acquired real properties in the books of the municipality upon securing proper and valid papers to support the documentation /titling.
Intensify the verification process and titling of the Land owing to the LGU and booked up in the LGU’s account the said lots to establish ownership and avoid the risk of court litigation from the previous owners who are interested to take back the properties.
Book up in the LGU books the correct land valuation in order   to present an accurate land account in the financial statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: 1. Require the inventory committee to submit the inventory report declaring the assets which are already obsolete, unserviceable and no longer used by the municipality to the municipal accountant to ascertain the adjustments effected in the PPE; 2. Conduct proper turnover of assets to the barangays for proper control and monitoring; 3. Require the municipal accountant to effect necessary re-adjustments based on the action taken by the Inventory Committee and the transfer of assets to the barangays.
observations.managementAction: Creation of new inventory committee has made the actual inventory. The Committee made the list but the actual report is not yet submitted. Some adjustments are being made.
observations.reason: List is already provided but the actual report is not yet submitted; no adjustments made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: 1. Require the Property and Supply Office/Unit to maintain Stock Cards and reconcile the balances with the ledger cards of the Accounting Unit; 2. Require the Requisitioning unit to prepare the Requisition and Issue Slip of the items requisitioned; 3. Require the Property and Supply Office/Unit to release the items based on the approved RIS and prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) for submission to the Accounting Section; 4. Require the accounting section to record its issuances based on the SSMI.
observations.managementAction: The Property and Supply Office/Unit to maintain stock cards; Requisitioning officers will prepare RIS; the Accounting Section will record issuances based on SSMI. Some implementation done; purchases were recorded based on acceptance.
observations.reason: No stock cards maintained previously; acceptance report is basis of recording; some procedures now in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: 1. Require the municipal treasurer/general services officer to accept first all deliveries of items purchased by the local government unit before the requesting officer accepts; 2. Require the municipal treasurer to prepare the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) in the issuance of supplies and materials acknowledged received by the requisitioning officer/employee; 3. Require the municipal treasurer to summarize weekly the RIS using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit to the municipal accountant for recording; 4. Require the municipal accountant to record the expenditures based on the SSMI using the appropriate expenditure accounts in the books of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Requesting Officer is to accept first the deliveries of items purchased by the LGU. The Municipal Treasurer will prepare RIS and SSMI for submission to the Accounting Unit. Expenditures were recorded based on acceptance and SSMI.
observations.reason: No GSO designated yet to accept deliveries initially; RIS and SSMI now being prepared but not consistently.

observations.recommendation: 1. Require the driver/operator to prepare the Waste Materials Report (WMR) and turn over the damaged parts to the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer for safekeeping before requesting the new parts; 2. Require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to receive the damaged parts and keep in a safe place for proper control and disposal.
observations.managementAction: We required the driver/mechanic concerned to prepare the Waste Materials Report and request for spare parts. The Property Officer is the custodian of the damaged parts.
observations.reason: Report attached but not in proper format; acceptance report not prepared.

observations.recommendation: a. The concerned personnel of the Property/Supply Unit be instructed to immediately prepare/renew all Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) and every three (3) years thereafter pursuant to Section 42 of the NGAS Manual; b. Submit copies of the ARE to this Office for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Property Officer was instructed to prepare and renew the Acknowledgement Receipts. The Property Officer was the custodian and will prepare the AREs. Some AREs were prepared/renewed and submitted.
observations.reason: AREs were prepared but not all were turned over; some were not prepared/renewed.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation and/or demand letters to individuals concerned. Otherwise, upon due notice, effect deduction from whatever benefits due the concerned official/employee. b. For receivables from officials and employees arising from overpayment of claims, the Municipal Treasurer shall institute deductions on their subsequent claims and salaries in satisfaction of their indebtedness pursuant to PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Some employees already refunded their disallowances and amounts deducted from their salaries on staggered basis.
observations.reason: Deductions were effected on salaries of concerned employees on staggered basis.

observations.recommendation: a. Instruct the GSO to strictly monitor the use of all government motor vehicles through proper and completely filled up driver’s trip tickets. b. Submit the required monthly reports on official travels and fuel consumption, pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: The GSO is to monitor use of vehicles and ensure driver’s trip tickets are filled; Biometrics installed to monitor attendance. The GSO will submit monthly reports.
observations.reason: No GSO installed earlier; now GSO functions being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Stop spending on personal, entertaining and extravagant disbursements pursuant to COA Circular 85-55A.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed to minimize entertaining disbursements; guests were minimized.
observations.reason: LGU still provides food for personnel and guests occasionally.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Improve the utilization of the heavy equipment and provide strategies to increase the rent income sufficient to cover the amortization costs to develop a sustainable economic enterprise of these heavy equipments.
observations.managementAction: No strategies were adopted. The BAC consolidates procurements and shopping were done for smaller quantities; some rentals continued.
observations.reason: No clear strategies adopted yet to increase utilization and income.

observations.recommendation: a. Adhere strictly to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly accounts and reports. b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly accounts to the Office of the Auditor promptly after these are recorded/posted in the General and Subsidiary Ledgers so that the Auditor could conduct the required review and verification.
observations.managementAction: Coordination between offices; the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the time frame. Some documents submitted upon request from COA; delays occurred when Municipal Accountant was detailed to Market Section.
observations.reason: Delayed submission due to voluminous transactions and lack of manpower; some reports submitted upon COA request.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Strictly comply with Section 10 of RA 9184 by requiring that all procurements should be effected through public bidding, while allowing exceptions only in justifiable cases.
observations.managementAction: The BAC already consolidates purchases; shopping done for smaller quantities and replenishments; BAC will revise procedures.
observations.reason: Shopping still done for small purchases; BAC consolidates transactions and is addressing the issue.
